summary: SALV (Smart Asset Life-Cycle Vault) Phase 1 MVP — full backend + frontend e2e testing complete. Backend pytest 23/23 PASSED covering vault auto-creation, settings PATCH, DMS check-in, asset CRUD, verify resets timer, due-soon, beneficiary share validation (≤100%), handoff (400 no benef / 200 success), admin scan (403 non-admin / 200 admin), unauthenticated 401, asset cascade delete. Frontend /asset-vault flows verified end-to-end: ProtectedRoute redirects to /login when unauth (vault-login-required only renders when component mounted via auth race — main path is ProtectedRoute redirect), stat cards render correct counts, dms-card + check-in-btn updates timer, add-asset-modal → submit-add-asset creates asset, asset-row click reveals asset-detail-* with reverify-btn / trigger-handoff-btn / delete-asset-btn / add-beneficiary-btn, add-beneficiary-form → submit-beneficiary-btn appends beneficiary-{id}, settings-modal saves vault name + DMS interval. No critical or blocking issues found.

issues: React hydration warnings printed to console: 'In HTML, <span> cannot be a child of <select>' and '<span> cannot be a child of <option>'. Caused by Emergent's data-ve-dynamic instrumentation wrapping <select>/<option> children with <span style=display:contents>. Not from app code — purely cosmetic, no functional impact.

critical_code_review_comments: salv_routes.py: ProtectedRoute on the frontend already redirects unauthenticated users to /login, so the [data-testid=vault-login-required] code path inside AssetVault.jsx will rarely render in production. Component still exposes the testid for the brief unauthenticated render window — OK.

GET /assets/{id}: admin role bypasses 403 (allowed). PATCH/DELETE/verify/add-beneficiary ENDPOINTS DO NOT honor admin bypass — only owner. Inconsistency: read+handoff allow admin, write does not. Consider documenting/aligning.
Beneficiary share validation only checks per-asset total ≤ 100%, but does not prevent share_percent=0 (allowed by Field(ge=0)). A 0% beneficiary is meaningless — consider gt=0.
trigger-handoff: marks beneficiaries pending→notified, but if some are already 'notified' from a prior handoff, the count returned (len(beneficiaries)) overstates fresh notifications. Not blocking.

admin/scan: only emits asset_overdue events but does not change asset status to 'overdue' in DB. So the client must re-query and compute overdue from next_verification_at — fine, but document expectation.
Settings PATCH does not validate name length (could be empty string after strip). Not security-critical.

AssetVault.jsx: `const dmsColor = dms.status === 'ok' ? 'emerald' : ...` is interpolated into Tailwind class names (`bg-${dmsColor}-500/5`). Tailwind JIT may purge these dynamic classes if not safelisted — verified visually on demo (emerald shown OK). If status flips to warning/triggered the colors may not appear. Recommend safelisting amber-500/red-500 variants.
AssetVault.jsx Modal click-outside dismisses modal even when user is mid-form (fields will be lost). Minor UX.
No test for blockchain_seal auto-link (requires existing seal hash) — left out of pytest because no seeded seal.
test_credentials: admin@notarychain.com / Admin123! and demo@test.com / Demo123! (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Both worked. Login API returns {access_token, token_type}.
seed_data_creation: Pytest creates and self-cleans TEST_-prefixed assets and beneficiaries. UI run left 1 beneficiary 'TEST UI Heir <uiheir@test.com>' attached to a leftover asset from the main agent's manual curl ('123 Main St Deed $750,000'). Vault is renamed to 'TEST UI Vault' with DMS=150 days for demo@test.com. Safe to leave or clean via a fresh PATCH /api/salv/vault.
context_for_next_testing_agent: SALV Phase 1 verified. Backend tests: pytest backend/tests/test_salv.py -v. Endpoints under /api/salv/*. Vault auto-creates on first GET /vault for the JWT user. Pydantic Field range 30–3650 for both DMS days and verification_interval_days returns 422 (NOT 400) — make sure tests assert 422 for range violations and 400 only for explicit HTTPException paths (asset_type enum, missing title, bad hash length, exceeding 100% share, no beneficiaries on handoff). Admin role bypass exists for GET /assets/{id} and trigger-handoff but not for PATCH/DELETE/verify/beneficiary creation. Frontend testids verified: asset-vault-page, vault-login-required, vault-name, stat-assets, stat-beneficiaries, stat-due-soon, stat-value, dms-card, check-in-btn, add-asset-btn, add-asset-modal, asset-type-select, asset-title-input, asset-description-input, asset-value-input, asset-jurisdiction-input, asset-hash-input, asset-interval-input, submit-add-asset, asset-row-{id}, asset-detail-{id}, close-asset-detail, reverify-btn, trigger-handoff-btn, delete-asset-btn, add-beneficiary-btn, add-beneficiary-form, beneficiary-name-input, beneficiary-email-input, beneficiary-relationship-input, beneficiary-share-input, submit-beneficiary-btn, beneficiary-{id}, remove-beneficiary-{id}, open-settings-btn, settings-modal, settings-name-input, settings-days-input, settings-save-btn, modal-close, assets-list, assets-empty, vault-loading, vault-error.
verified_flows: GET /api/salv/vault (user) → auto-creates default vault, returns vault+dms+stats+assets[]+beneficiaries[]
POST /api/salv/assets valid deed → 200, status=active, next_verification_at = last_verified_at + interval days
Frontend /asset-vault unauthenticated → ProtectedRoute redirects to /login (no vault-login-required render)
Frontend /asset-vault authenticated → asset-vault-page renders with all 4 stat cards, dms-card OK with check-in-btn
Frontend add-asset-btn → add-asset-modal opens; fill title+value+jurisdiction; submit-add-asset → modal closes, asset added, list refreshed
Frontend asset-row click → asset-detail-{id} panel with reverify-btn/trigger-handoff-btn/delete-asset-btn/add-beneficiary-btn all present and functional
Frontend add-beneficiary-btn → add-beneficiary-form; submit-beneficiary-btn → appends beneficiary-{id} row
Frontend open-settings-btn → settings-modal; save updates vault-name display
rca of the issue: No bugs requiring fix. Two console hydration warnings observed (<span> child of <select>/<option>) originate from Emergent runtime instrumentation wrapping JSX with data-ve-dynamic spans, not from AssetVault.jsx. They are noisy in dev console but do not break the dropdown — submit-add-asset succeeds with correct asset_type. Reproduction: open /asset-vault → click 'Add asset' → React DOM hydration warning logged. Mitigation (optional): replace native <select>/<option> with the shadcn Select component to bypass the instrumentation collision.
